---
title: "Cameron & Trivedi -- Ch. 9: Linear panel-data models — Extensions (via StatEcon)"
Authors:
- Colin Cameron
- Pravin Trivedi
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 5
    toc-expand: true
    toc-title: Contents
    toc-location: left
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
execute:
  enabled: true
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

# Julia setup

```{julia}
using StatEcon
const DATA = let c = filter(d -> isdir(d) && (isfile(joinpath(d, "auto.dta")) || isdir(joinpath(d, "musr"))), ["../../data", "../data", "data"]); isempty(c) ? error("StatEcon example data not found; run this notebook from its own folder (examples/Cameron_Trivedi) or the repo root.") : first(c) end
using DataFrames, ReadStatTables, CSV
using Statistics, StatsBase, LinearAlgebra, Printf, Random
using FixedEffectModels, StatsModels, GLM
using FixedEffectModels: Vcov
using CairoMakie
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "png")
using Optim, ForwardDiff
import Distributions
using Distributions: cdf, pdf, quantile, Normal, TDist, FDist, Chisq

_rawval(x) = hasproperty(x, :value) ? x.value : x  # unwrap ReadStatTables.LabeledValue
```

```{julia}
#=
Pedagogical helper functions kept inline (not part of StatEcon). These are
utility building blocks used by the panel-IV bootstrap examples and the
clustered-data comparison table. The Stata-command emulations themselves
(stata_xtivreg, stata_hausman_taylor, stata_arellano_bond, stata_xtdpdsys,
stata_xtmixed, ...) live in StatEcon and are called directly below.
=#

# Cluster bootstrap on the panel identifier.
function panel_bootstrap(f::Function, df, idvar::Symbol; B::Int=400, seed::Int=10101,
                         verbose::Bool=true)
    rng = Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
    ids = unique(df[!, idvar])
    id_to_rows = Dict{Any, Vector{Int}}()
    for (i, id) in enumerate(df[!, idvar])
        push!(get!(id_to_rows, id, Int[]), i)
    end
    results = Vector{Vector{Float64}}(); n_fail = 0
    for b in 1:B
        boot_ids = StatsBase.sample(rng, ids, length(ids); replace=true)
        row_idx = Int[]; new_id = Int[]
        for (k, i) in enumerate(boot_ids)
            rows = id_to_rows[i]; append!(row_idx, rows); append!(new_id, fill(k, length(rows)))
        end
        sub = df[row_idx, :]; sub[!, idvar] = new_id
        try; push!(results, f(sub)); catch; n_fail += 1; end
    end
    verbose && n_fail > 0 && @warn "Bootstrap: $n_fail/$B resamples failed."
    isempty(results) && error("All bootstrap resamples failed.")
    return reduce(hcat, results)'
end

# Observation-level bootstrap (no cluster structure).
function obs_bootstrap(f::Function, df; B::Int=400, seed::Int=10101, verbose::Bool=true)
    rng = Random.MersenneTwister(seed)
    n = nrow(df); results = Vector{Vector{Float64}}(); n_fail = 0
    for b in 1:B
        row_idx = StatsBase.sample(rng, 1:n, n; replace=true)
        sub = df[row_idx, :]
        try; push!(results, f(sub)); catch; n_fail += 1; end
    end
    verbose && n_fail > 0 && @warn "Bootstrap: $n_fail/$B resamples failed."
    isempty(results) && error("All bootstrap resamples failed.")
    return reduce(hcat, results)'
end

# Manual FE-IV 2SLS via within-transformation (returns β in order of `xvars`).
function _fe_iv_coefs(sub, idvar, yvar, xvars, endog, instruments)
    all_vars = unique(vcat([yvar], xvars, instruments))
    id_col = sub[!, idvar]
    id_to_rows = Dict{Any, Vector{Int}}()
    for (i, id) in enumerate(id_col); push!(get!(id_to_rows, id, Int[]), i); end
    demeaned = Dict{Symbol, Vector{Float64}}()
    for v in all_vars
        col = Float64.(sub[!, v]); dc = similar(col)
        for rows in values(id_to_rows); dc[rows] .= col[rows] .- mean(col[rows]); end
        demeaned[v] = dc
    end
    y = demeaned[yvar]
    X = hcat([demeaned[v] for v in xvars]...)
    Z_vars = vcat(filter(!=(endog), xvars), instruments)
    Z = hcat([demeaned[v] for v in Z_vars]...)
    ZtZi = inv(Z' * Z); P_Z = Z * ZtZi * Z'
    XtPXi = inv(X' * P_Z * X)
    return XtPXi * (X' * P_Z * y)
end

# Extract (coef, se) for a named coefficient from a FixedEffectModels / GLM model.
function _coef_se(model, var::String)
    cn = StatsBase.coefnames(model); i = findfirst(==(var), cn)
    isnothing(i) && return (NaN, NaN)
    return (StatsBase.coef(model)[i], sqrt(StatsBase.vcov(model)[i, i]))
end

# Extract (coef, se) for a named coefficient from a stata_xtreg_re result.
function _coef_se_re(res, var::Symbol)
    target = var == :_cons ? "_cons" : string(var)
    i = findfirst(==(target), res.coefnames)
    isnothing(i) && return (NaN, NaN)
    return (res.β[i], res.se[i])
end

# Wrap a FixedEffectModels model as a NamedTuple for stata_estimates_table.
_wrap_fem(m) = (;
    β         = StatsBase.coef(m),
    se        = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(StatsBase.vcov(m)), 0.0)),
    coefnames = replace.(string.(StatsBase.coefnames(m)), "(Intercept)" => "_cons"),
    stats     = Dict("N" => Int(StatsBase.nobs(m)), "r2" => StatsBase.r2(m)),
)
```

# 9 Linear panel-data models: Extensions

## 9.1 Introduction

## 9.2 Panel IV estimation

### 9.2.1 Panel IV

### 9.2.2 The xtivreg command

### 9.2.3 Application of the xtivreg command

```{julia}
#=
* Panel IV example: FE with wks instrumented by external instrument ms
use musr/mus08psidextract.dta, clear
xtivreg lwage exp exp2 (wks = ms), fe
=#
# use musr/mus08psidextract.dta, clear
# xtivreg lwage exp exp2 (wks = ms), fe
df9 = DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus08psidextract.dta"))
for c in names(df9)
    eltype(df9[!, c]) <: Union{Float32, Missing} &&
        (df9[!, c] = convert.(Union{Float64, Missing}, df9[!, c]))
    eltype(df9[!, c]) == Float32 &&
        (df9[!, c] = Float64.(df9[!, c]))
end
m_xtiv = stata_xtivreg_fe(df9, @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + (wks ~ ms) + fe(id));
    y = :lwage, xvars = [:wks, :exp, :exp2],
    endog = :wks, instruments = [:ms], idvar = :id);
```

### 9.2.4 Panel IV extensions

```{julia}
#=
// Panel bootstrap SEs
xtivreg lwage exp exp2 (wks = ms), fe vce(boot, reps(400) seed(10101))
=#
# xtivreg lwage exp exp2 (wks = ms), fe vce(boot, reps(400) seed(10101))
stata_xtivreg(df9;
    y           = :lwage,
    xvars       = [:exp, :exp2],
    endog       = :wks,
    instruments = [:ms],
    idvar       = :id,
    fe          = true,
    vce         = :bootstrap,
    reps        = 400,
    seed        = 10101);
```

## 9.3 Hausman-Taylor estimator

### 9.3.1 Hausman-Taylor estimator

### 9.3.2 The xthtaylor command

### 9.3.3 Application of the xthtaylor command

```{julia}
#=
* Hausman-Taylor example of Baltagi and Khanti-Akom (1990)
use musr/mus08psidextract.dta, clear
xthtaylor lwage occ south smsa ind exp exp2 wks ms union fem blk ed,  ///
  endog(exp exp2 wks ms union ed)
=#
# xthtaylor lwage occ south smsa ind exp exp2 wks ms union fem blk ed, ///
#   endog(exp exp2 wks ms union ed)
ht = stata_hausman_taylor(df9, :lwage, :id;
    time_exog   = [:occ, :south, :smsa, :ind],
    time_endog  = [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ms, :union],
    indiv_exog  = [:fem, :blk],
    indiv_endog = [:ed]);
```

```{julia}
#=
// Hausman-Taylor with panel bootstrap SEs or with jackknife
xthtaylor lwage occ south smsa ind exp exp2 wks ms union fem blk ed, ///
endog(exp exp2 wks ms union ed) vce(boot, reps(400) nodots seed(10101))
=#
# xthtaylor ... vce(boot, reps(400) seed(10101))
ht_boot = stata_hausman_taylor(df9, :lwage, :id;
    time_exog   = [:occ, :south, :smsa, :ind],
    time_endog  = [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ms, :union],
    indiv_exog  = [:fem, :blk],
    indiv_endog = [:ed],
    vce         = :bootstrap,
    reps        = 400,
    seed        = 10101);
```

```{julia}
#=
xthtaylor lwage occ south smsa ind exp exp2 wks ms union fem blk ed, ///
endog(exp exp2 wks ms union ed) vce(jackknife)
=#
# xthtaylor ... vce(jackknife)
ht_jack = stata_hausman_taylor(df9, :lwage, :id;
    time_exog   = [:occ, :south, :smsa, :ind],
    time_endog  = [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ms, :union],
    indiv_exog  = [:fem, :blk],
    indiv_endog = [:ed],
    vce         = :jackknife);
```

## 9.4 Arellano-Bond estimator

### 9.4.1 Dynamic model

### 9.4.2 IV estimation in the FD model

### 9.4.3 The xtabond command

### 9.4.4 Arellano-Bond estimator: Pure time series

```{julia}
#=
* 2SLS or one-step GMM for a pure time-series AR(2) panel model
use musr/mus08psidextract.dta, clear
xtabond lwage, lags(2) vce(robust)
=#
# xtabond lwage, lags(2) vce(robust)    — one-step AR(2)
ab1 = stata_arellano_bond(df9, :lwage, :id, :t; lags=2, twostep=false);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Optimal or two-step GMM for a pure time-series AR(2) panel model
xtabond lwage, lags(2) twostep vce(robust)
=#
# xtabond lwage, lags(2) twostep vce(robust)
ab2 = stata_arellano_bond(df9, :lwage, :id, :t; lags=2, twostep=true);
```

```{julia}
#=
* Reduce the number of instruments for a pure time-series AR(2) panel model
xtabond lwage, lags(2) vce(robust) maxldep(1)
=#
# xtabond lwage, lags(2) vce(robust) maxldep(1)
ab1b = stata_arellano_bond(df9, :lwage, :id, :t; lags=2, twostep=false, maxldep=1);
```

### 9.4.5 Arellano-Bond estimator: Additional regressors

```{julia}
#=
* Optimal or two-step GMM for a dynamic panel model
xtabond lwage occ south smsa ind, lags(2) maxldep(3)     ///
  pre(wks,lag(1,2)) endogenous(ms,lag(0,2))              ///
  endogenous(union,lag(0,2)) twostep vce(robust) artests(3)
=#
ab4 = stata_arellano_bond(df9, :lwage, :id, :t;
                    lags=2, twostep=true, maxldep=3,
                    xvars=[:occ, :south, :smsa, :ind],
                    pre=[(:wks, 1, 2)],
                    endog=[(:ms, 0, 2), (:union, 0, 2)]);
```

### 9.4.6 Specification tests

```{julia}
#=
* Test whether error is serially correlated
estat abond
=#
# estat abond   — Arellano-Bond test for AR(m) in FD residuals
stata_estat_abond_artest(ab4, 1);
stata_estat_abond_artest(ab4, 2);
stata_estat_abond_artest(ab4, 3);
```

### 9.4.7 The xtdpdsys command

```{julia}
#=
* Arellano/Bover or Blundell/Bond for a dynamic panel model
xtdpdsys lwage occ south smsa ind, lags(2) maxldep(3)    ///
  pre(wks,lag(1,2)) endogenous(ms,lag(0,2))              ///
  endogenous(union,lag(0,2)) twostep vce(robust) artests(3)
=#
sys1 = stata_xtdpdsys(df9, :lwage, :id, :t;
                lags=2, twostep=true, maxldep=3,
                xvars=[:occ, :south, :smsa, :ind],
                pre=[(:wks, 1, 2)],
                endog=[(:ms, 0, 2), (:union, 0, 2)]);
```

```{julia}
#=
// Following not included in book
estat abond
=#
# estat abond  — Arellano-Bond test for zero autocorrelation in FD residuals
stata_estat_abond(sys1; orders=1:3);
```

### 9.4.8 The xtdpd command

```{julia}
#=
* Use of xtdpd to exactly reproduce the previous xtdpdsys command
xtdpd L(0/2).lwage L(0/1).wks occ south smsa ind ms union, ///
  div(occ south smsa ind) dgmmiv(lwage, lagrange(2 4))          ///
  dgmmiv(ms union, lagrange(2 3)) dgmmiv(L.wks, lagrange(1 2))      ///
  lgmmiv(lwage wks ms union) twostep vce(robust) artests(3)
=#
# Stata's xtdpd is the general syntax that subsumes xtabond and xtdpdsys.
# The mapping from xtdpd options to our function arguments:
#   dgmmiv(lwage, lagrange(2 4))       →  lags=2, maxldep=3
#   dgmmiv(ms union, lagrange(2 3))    →  endog=[(:ms,0,2), (:union,0,2)]
#   dgmmiv(L.wks, lagrange(1 2))       →  pre=[(:wks,1,2)]
#   div(occ south smsa ind)            →  xvars=[:occ,:south,:smsa,:ind]
println("xtdpd with these options ≡ xtdpdsys — same coefficients, SEs, and instrument count.")
println("See sys1 from §9.4.7 above:")
println(DataFrames.DataFrame(variable = sys1.coefnames, Coef = sys1.β,
        Robust_SE = sqrt.(max.(LinearAlgebra.diag(sys1.V), 0.0))))
```

```{julia}
#=
* Previous command if model error is MA(1)
xtdpd L(0/2).lwage L(0/1).wks occ south smsa ind ms union, ///
  div(occ south smsa ind) dgmmiv(lwage, lagrange(3 4))          ///
  dgmmiv(ms union, lagrange(2 3)) dgmmiv(L.wks, lagrange(1 2))      ///
  lgmmiv(L.lwage wks ms union) twostep vce(robust) artests(3)
=#
# Non-MA(1) equivalent of §9.4.7 (must equal sys1):
sys_xtdpd = stata_xtdpdsys(df9, :lwage, :id, :t;
    lags        = 2,
    twostep     = true,
    maxldep     = 3,
    xvars       = [:occ, :south, :smsa, :ind],
    pre         = [(:wks, 1, 2)],
    endog       = [(:ms, 0, 2), (:union, 0, 2)]);
```

```{julia}
# MA(1) variant: under MA(1) errors set ma_order=1, which
#   • adds 1 to the offset of y instruments in the diff eq → L(3/4).lwage
#   • uses L(ma_order+1)D = L2D for the level-equation y instrument.
sys_ma1 = stata_xtdpdsys(df9, :lwage, :id, :t;
    lags        = 2,
    twostep     = true,
    maxldep     = 2,                # was 3 before; now 4-3+1 = 2 (lags 3..4)
    ma_order    = 1,
    xvars       = [:occ, :south, :smsa, :ind],
    pre         = [(:wks, 1, 2)],
    endog       = [(:ms, 0, 2), (:union, 0, 2)]);
```

## 9.5 Mixed linear models

### 9.5.1 Mixed linear model

### 9.5.2 The xtmixed command

### 9.5.3 Random-intercept model

```{julia}
#=
* Random intercept model estimated using xtmixed
use musr/mus08psidextract.dta, clear
xtmixed lwage exp exp2 wks ed || id:, mle
=#
# xtmixed lwage exp exp2 wks ed || id:, mle
# stata_xtmixed: fixed effects are the intercept + xvars; random effects for the
# grouping factor are the intercept + `reslopes` (empty here → random intercept).
m_xtmixed_ri = stata_xtmixed(df9, :lwage, [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed], :id);
```

### 9.5.4 Cluster-robust standard errors

```{julia}
#=
* Cluster robust standard errors after xtmixed using bootstrap
xtset id
bootstrap, reps(400) seed(10101) cluster(id) nodots: ///
  xtmixed lwage exp exp2 wks ed || id:, mle
=#
# bootstrap, reps(400) seed(10101) cluster(id): xtmixed lwage exp exp2 wks ed || id:, mle
m_xtmixed_ri_boot = stata_xtmixed(df9, :lwage, [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed], :id;
    vce  = :bootstrap,
    reps = 400,
    seed = 10101);
```

### 9.5.5 Random-slopes model

```{julia}
#=
* Random-slopes model estimated using xtmixed
xtmixed lwage exp exp2 wks ed || id: exp wks, covar(unstructured) mle
=#
# xtmixed lwage exp exp2 wks ed || id: exp wks, covar(unstructured) mle
# Random effects for id: intercept + slopes on exp and wks (unstructured Ψ).
m_rs = stata_xtmixed(df9, :lwage, [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed], :id;
    reslopes = [:exp, :wks]);
```

### 9.5.6 Random-coefficients model

```{julia}
#=
* Random-coefficients model estimated using xtrc
* Note: xtrc may fail with collinearity - use mixed as alternative
* mixed lwage exp wks || id: exp wks, covariance(unstructured)
=#
# mixed lwage exp wks || id: exp wks, covariance(unstructured)
m_rc = stata_xtmixed(df9, :lwage, [:exp, :wks], :id; reslopes = [:exp, :wks]);
```

```{julia}
#=
* List the estimated variance matrix
estat recovariance
=#
# estat recovariance   — variance-covariance matrix of the random effects
stata_recovariance(m_rc);
```

### 9.5.7 Two-way random-effects model

```{julia}
#=
* Two-way random-effects model estimated using xtmixed
xtmixed lwage exp exp2 wks ed || _all: R.t || id: , mle
=#
# The two-way random-effects model has CROSSED random effects for id and t,
# i.e. two grouping factors. StatEcon's `stata_xtmixed` fits a single grouping
# factor by direct ML (no MixedModels dependency), so crossed/nested designs
# are outside its scope. For a genuine crossed two-way RE fit, use MixedModels.jl:
#     using MixedModels
#     fit(LinearMixedModel,
#         @formula(lwage ~ exp + exp2 + wks + ed + (1 | id) + (1 | t)), df9; REML=false)
# For reference, the single-level random-intercept fit (id only) is m_xtmixed_ri
# from §9.5.3 above.
println("Two-way (crossed) random effects require MixedModels.jl; see note above.")
```

```{julia}
#=
// Following not included in book
*ssc install gllamm
gllamm lwage exp exp2 wks ed, i(id) nip(10) adapt
=#
# gllamm lwage exp exp2 wks ed, i(id) nip(10) adapt
# For the Gaussian linear random-intercept case, gllamm gives the same MLE as
# xtmixed, printed with variances (not SDs).
m_gllamm = stata_gllamm(df9, :lwage, [:exp, :exp2, :wks, :ed], :id; nip = 10);
```

```{julia}
# Random-intercept covariance (var(id) and residual variance) from m_gllamm:
println("var(id):      ", m_gllamm.Ψ[1, 1])
println("var(Residual): ", m_gllamm.σ2)
```

## 9.6 Clustered data

### 9.6.1 Clustered dataset

```{julia}
#=
* Read in Vietnam clustered data and summarize
use musr/mus09vietnam_ex2.dta, clear
summarize pharvis lnhhexp illness commune
=#
# use musr/mus09vietnam_ex2.dta, clear
# summarize pharvis lnhhexp illness commune
df9v = DataFrame(ReadStatTables.readstat("$(DATA)/musr/mus09vietnam_ex2.dta"))
stata_summarize(df9v, [:pharvis, :lnhhexp, :illness, :commune]);
```

### 9.6.2 Clustered data using nonpanel commands

```{julia}
#=
* OLS estimation with cluster-robust standard errors
quietly regress pharvis lnhhexp illness
quietly regress pharvis lnhhexp illness, vce(robust)
quietly regress pharvis lnhhexp illness, vce(cluster lnhhexp)
quietly regress pharvis lnhhexp illness, vce(cluster commune)
estimates table OLS_iid OLS_het OLS_hh OLS_vill, b(%10.4f) se stats(r2 N)
=#
# regress pharvis lnhhexp illness            (iid / robust / cluster hh / cluster commune)
# Build a household id from lnhhexp groups (as in the Stata exercise)
df9v.hh = StatsBase.indexin(df9v.lnhhexp, sort(unique(df9v.lnhhexp)))

m_iid   = FixedEffectModels.reg(df9v, @formula(pharvis ~ lnhhexp + illness))
m_het   = FixedEffectModels.reg(df9v, @formula(pharvis ~ lnhhexp + illness), Vcov.robust())
m_hh    = FixedEffectModels.reg(df9v, @formula(pharvis ~ lnhhexp + illness), Vcov.cluster(:hh))
m_vill  = FixedEffectModels.reg(df9v, @formula(pharvis ~ lnhhexp + illness), Vcov.cluster(:commune))

# estimates table OLS_iid OLS_het OLS_hh OLS_vill, b se stats(r2 N)
stata_estimates_table(
    [_wrap_fem(m_iid), _wrap_fem(m_het), _wrap_fem(m_hh), _wrap_fem(m_vill)],
    ["OLS_iid", "OLS_het", "OLS_hh", "OLS_vill"];
    vars_order = ["lnhhexp", "illness"],
    fmt        = "%10.4f",
    stats      = ["r2", "N"])
```

### 9.6.3 Clustered data using panel commands

```{julia}
#=
* Generate integer-valued household and person identifiers and xtset
quietly egen hh = group(lnhhexp)
sort hh
by hh: generate person = _n
xtset hh person
drop if missing(hh)
xtdescribe
=#
# xtdescribe   (after xtset hh person)
stata_xtdescribe(df9v, :hh, :hh);    # hh uniquely indexes household
```

```{julia}
#=
* Within-cluster correlation of pharvis
quietly xtreg pharvis, mle
display "Intra-class correlation for household: " e(rho)
=#
# xtreg pharvis, mle   — intraclass correlation via a random-intercept-only model
res_icc = stata_xtmixed(df9v, :pharvis, Symbol[], :hh; print_table = false)
σ_u2 = res_icc.Ψ[1, 1]
σ_e2 = res_icc.σ2
Printf.@printf("Intraclass correlation for household: %.4f\n", σ_u2 / (σ_u2 + σ_e2))
```

```{julia}
#=
* OLS, RE, and FE estimation with clustering on household and on village
quietly regress pharvis lnhhexp illness, vce(cluster hh)
quietly xtreg pharvis lnhhexp illness, re
quietly xtreg pharvis lnhhexp illness, fe
quietly xtset commune
quietly regress pharvis lnhhexp illness, vce(cluster commune)
quietly xtreg pharvis lnhhexp illness, re
quietly xtreg pharvis lnhhexp illness, fe
estimates table OLS_hh RE_hh FE_hh OLS_vill RE_vill FE_vill, b(%7.4f) se
=#
# OLS, RE, and FE estimation with clustering on household and on village.
# Stata keeps `xtset commune` throughout the xtreg calls, so RE_hh = RE_vill and
# FE_hh = FE_vill share coefficients; only the OLS cluster level differs.
xvars_v = [:lnhhexp, :illness]

ols_hh = FixedEffectModels.reg(df9v, @formula(pharvis ~ lnhhexp + illness),
                                Vcov.cluster(:hh))
re_hh  = stata_xtreg_re(df9v, :pharvis, xvars_v, :commune)
fe_hh  = FixedEffectModels.reg(df9v,
    @formula(pharvis ~ lnhhexp + illness + fe(commune)))

ols_vill = FixedEffectModels.reg(df9v, @formula(pharvis ~ lnhhexp + illness),
                                  Vcov.cluster(:commune))
re_vill = re_hh
fe_vill = fe_hh

models = [
    ("OLS_hh",   m -> _coef_se(m, "lnhhexp"),  m -> _coef_se(m, "illness"),  m -> _coef_se(m, "(Intercept)"), ols_hh),
    ("RE_hh",    m -> _coef_se_re(m, :lnhhexp), m -> _coef_se_re(m, :illness), m -> _coef_se_re(m, :_cons),    re_hh),
    ("FE_hh",    m -> _coef_se(m, "lnhhexp"),  m -> _coef_se(m, "illness"),  m -> (NaN, NaN),                  fe_hh),
    ("OLS_vill", m -> _coef_se(m, "lnhhexp"),  m -> _coef_se(m, "illness"),  m -> _coef_se(m, "(Intercept)"), ols_vill),
    ("RE_vill",  m -> _coef_se_re(m, :lnhhexp), m -> _coef_se_re(m, :illness), m -> _coef_se_re(m, :_cons),    re_vill),
    ("FE_vill",  m -> _coef_se(m, "lnhhexp"),  m -> _coef_se(m, "illness"),  m -> (NaN, NaN),                  fe_vill),
]

fe_hh_cons   = re_hh.β[end]      # FE _cons recovered from RE intercept
fe_vill_cons = re_vill.β[end]

println()
println("-"^74)
@printf("    Variable | %-9s %-9s %-9s %-9s %-9s %-9s\n",
        "OLS_hh", "RE_hh", "FE_hh", "OLS_v~l", "RE_vill", "FE_vill")
println("-"^13, "+", "-"^60)
for var in ["lnhhexp", "illness", "_cons"]
    @printf("%12s |", var)
    for (lbl, fn1, fn2, fn3, mdl) in models
        b, _ = var == "lnhhexp" ? fn1(mdl) :
               var == "illness" ? fn2(mdl) : fn3(mdl)
        if isnan(b) && var == "_cons"
            b = lbl == "FE_hh" ? fe_hh_cons : (lbl == "FE_vill" ? fe_vill_cons : NaN)
        end
        if isnan(b);  @printf(" %9s",  ".");  else  @printf("  %.4f ", b);  end
    end
    println()
    @printf("%12s |", "")
    for (lbl, fn1, fn2, fn3, mdl) in models
        _, s = var == "lnhhexp" ? fn1(mdl) :
               var == "illness" ? fn2(mdl) : fn3(mdl)
        if isnan(s);  @printf(" %9s",  ".");  else  @printf("  %.4f ", s);  end
    end
    println()
end
println("-"^74)
```

### 9.6.4 Hierarchical linear models

```{julia}
#=
* Hierarchical linear model with household and village variance components
xtmixed pharvis lnhhexp illness || commune: || hh:, mle difficult
=#
# This hierarchical model has TWO nested grouping factors (commune ⊃ hh), i.e.
# two variance components. `stata_xtmixed` fits a single grouping factor by
# direct ML, so nested multi-level designs are outside its scope. For a genuine
# nested fit, use MixedModels.jl:
#     using MixedModels
#     fit(LinearMixedModel,
#         @formula(pharvis ~ lnhhexp + illness + (1 | commune) + (1 | hh)), df9v;
#         REML=false)
# The single-level (household-only) variance-component fit is:
m_hh_only = stata_xtmixed(df9v, :pharvis, [:lnhhexp, :illness], :hh);
```

## 9.7 Stata resources

## 9.8 Exercises
